message: Heads up — seeing elevated latency on the auth service. Token validation is taking 4-6s instead of the normal <200ms.
Users are getting logged out mid-session. Getting reports from customer success team — Salesforce integration is breaking because JWT refresh is failing.
How widespread? Is this affecting all users or a subset?
Looking at logs — hitting ~30% of auth requests. Seems correlated with users in the EU-West region. US users appear unaffected.
Datadog shows the Redis session cache in eu-west-1 is at 92% memory. I think it's evicting active sessions. That's the token validation lag.
The cache shouldn't be that full. Was there a deployment? @Jordan Kim did your team push anything to auth last night?
Yes — we deployed the new refresh token rotation feature at 23:45 UTC last night. It stores both old and new tokens during the rotation window. Could be doubling cache usage.
That's it. The rotation TTL is set to 24h — so EU-West has been accumulating paired tokens since midnight. Redis is basically double-loaded. Flushing expired keys now.
Customer success says 3 enterprise accounts have called in. What's our ETA on resolution? I need to notify the account managers.
Flush is ~60% done. Redis memory already down to 74%. Auth latency dropping — seeing p99 at 1.2s and falling. ETA to normal: 15-20 minutes.
We'll need to roll back the 24h rotation TTL to 1h as a hotfix. PR is ready — needs review and deploy. That's the permanent fix.
Reviewing PR now. Latency is back to 800ms. Getting better. Users should stop seeing logouts soon.
